title: draba rigida var. imbricata
intro: Draba rigida var. imbricata, also known as the alpine whitlow grass, is a plant species that belongs to the mustard family. It is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including skin infections, wounds, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial and expectorant properties. Herbal preparations of Draba rigida var. imbricata can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
